title: AI Will Transform Jobs, Not Simply Replace Them

sources.title: Generative AI and Jobs: A Refined Global Index of Occupational Exposure

claims.claim: Globally, one in four workers is in an occupation with some exposure to generative AI.
Only 3.3% of global employment falls into the highest exposure category.
Because most occupations contain tasks requiring human input, the ILO concludes job transformation is the most likely impact, not replacement.

scenes.narration: AI could affect one in four workers worldwide. But that does not mean one in four jobs will disappear.

visual.headline: AI will touch your job — not how you think

scenes.narration: Exposure is not replacement. A job is a bundle of tasks — and AI only touches some of them.

visual.subtitle: A job is a bundle of tasks. AI touches only some.

scenes.narration: The ILO measured it. Only 3.3 percent of global employment sits in the highest exposure category.

visual.label: of global employment in the highest exposure category
visual.source: SOURCE · ILO, GENERATIVE AI AND JOBS
visual.subtitle: Some exposure is common. Full exposure is rare.

scenes.narration: So the real split is not jobs that stay versus jobs that vanish. It is tasks AI absorbs... versus tasks that still need human judgment.

visual.subtitle: Most occupations still contain human-input tasks.

scenes.narration: So the real career risk is not AI taking your job. It is your job changing faster than your skills.

visual.setup: Jobs will not vanish. They will be rewritten.
visual.question: CAN YOUR SKILLS KEEP UP?
visual.subtitle: Transformation rewards those who adapt first.

packaging.youtubeTitle: AI May Change 1 in 4 Workers' Jobs — But Not How You Think
packaging.instagramCaption: One in four workers is in an occupation with some exposure to generative AI — but exposure is not the same as replacement. The ILO's task-level research points to transformation, not job loss. The real question: can your skills keep up? Source: ILO, Generative AI and Jobs. #futureofwork #ai #careers #jobmarket
packaging.tiktokCaption: AI exposure does not automatically mean job loss. The bigger shift is what your job asks you to do next. #futureofwork #ai #careers
